1990January 22, 1990

🇫🇷 Paris, France

Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Paris, France, January 22, 1990 -- February 1, 1990

Meeting URNurn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:meeting:plenary-1990-01-paris-france

16 Resolutions

58Plenary
Encourage member bodies to increase participation in draughting models
SC 4 encourages member bodies to consider increasing their participation in this work to match the stated needs of their industries.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
57Plenary
Consider current ISO/STEP parts division and numbering scheme tentative
The current ISO/STEP parts division and numbering scheme shall be considered tentative until a final recommendation is received from WG 1. For timely distribution to SC 4, this document must be rec...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
56Plenary
Define new versions of STEP as dated collections of Committee Draft parts
It is resolved that SC 4 shall create new versions of STEP where a version is defined as a specific, dated collection of Committee Draft parts that are advanced simultaneously to DIS.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
55Plenary
Adopt strategy to divide DP 10303 into smaller volumes
SC 4 adopts the general strategy for breaking the DP 10303 (SC 4 N38) into a number of smaller volumes, each to be processed separately through approval of a Committee Draft and then to be balloted...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
54Plenary
Replace term Application Profile with Application Protocol
The term "Application Profile" identified in SC 4 Resolution 34 (Tokyo #6) is to be replaced by the term "Application Protocol."
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
53Plenary
Welcome new work items following change of scope
In response to the changing of scope, SC 4 is ready to welcome new work items, namely: a) Industrial manufacturing management data b) Quality data c) Maintenance data Members of SC 4 are solicited ...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
52Plenary
Propose reorganising STEP work into new Working Groups
In response to the request from ISO/TC 184, and in order to comply with the new ISO/IEC rules, SC 4 proposes to reorganise its work on STEP into the following Working Groups: * Resource Models (ex ...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
51Plenary
Recommend creating ad hoc working group to select application protocol topics
SC 4 recommends the creation of a small ad hoc working group of SC 4 to select topics on which to develop application protocols. These topics should be circulated to national bodies for comment and...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
50Plenary
Produce Technical Reports documenting present state of STEP technology
SC 4 resolves to produce a series of Technical Reports which document the present state of STEP technology. The contents of the report are subject to change. In particular, this effort requires: a)...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
49Plenary
Establish SC 4 Project Management Advisory Group
An SC 4 Project Management Advisory Group shall be established to provide project management and technical strategy for product data work items. Terms of reference of the Project Management Advisor...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
48Plenary
Create Strategic Planning Advisory Group and accept France as convenor
An advisory group called "Strategic Planning" is to be created to advise the SC 4 chair on matters concerning strategic planning and coordination of SC 4 activities.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
47Plenary
Affirm formation of Library Structures project in WG 1
SC 4 affirms the formation of a new project within WG 1 to study the requirements and structure of libraries within STEP. The title of the project will be "Library Structures." Mr. P. Harrow (UK) a...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
46Plenary
Establish new Working Group for Standard Parts
Following the assignment of the accepted new work item on a standard parts library, ISO/TC 184/SC 4 establishes a new working group with the following title and task: TITLE: Standard for the Neutra...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
45Plenary
Recommend changing SC 4 title to include Global Manufacturing Programming Languages
SC 4 respectfully recommends to the ISO/TC 184 chair that the title of the SC 4 Committee be changed to "Industrial Data and Global Manufacturing Programming Languages."
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
44Plenary
Invite and solicit proposals from IEC organisations for cooperative development
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 invites and solicits proposals from IEC organisations and others for cooperative development and participative approval of the appropriate parts of STEP. In particular, we invite th...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
Acclamation
Acclamation
SC 4 wishes to thank France for hosting the meeting of the committee this week.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
ISO/TC 184/SC 4

Industrial data

Standardization of the content, meaning, structure, representation and quality management of information for engineered products throughout their lifecycle.

Committee

  • Secretariat: ANSI (Secretariat)
  • Chair: Kenneth Swope
  • Established: 1984
  • Standards: 822 published
  • Members: 21 (P), 14 (O)
